Description: Define proper relationships with eager loading and constraints for optimal queries
Do: Define relationships with return types. Use eager loading. Use withDefault for optional
Dont: Access relationships without eager loading (N+1) or define without return types

Description: Write optimized Eloquent queries avoiding common performance pitfalls
Do: Use select() for needed columns. Use chunk/cursor for large datasets. Use raw for complex queries
Dont: Use get()/all() on large tables. Select all columns when you need two

Description: Configure Doctrine entities with proper mapping relationships and lifecycle
Do: Use PHP 8 attributes for mapping. Define bidirectional relationships carefully. Use cascade options
Dont: Use annotations (deprecated) or lazy-load collections without pagination

Description: Use Doctrine QueryBuilder for complex queries with proper joins and conditions
Do: Use QueryBuilder for reusable queries. Use DQL for complex queries. Hydrate to DTOs for read-only
Dont: Write raw SQL or load full entities for read-only lists
Code Good:
class OrderRepository extends ServiceEntityRepository {
    public function findRecentByUser(int $userId, int $days = 30): array {
        return $this->createQueryBuilder('o')
            ->select('o', 'i', 'p')
            ->leftJoin('o.items', 'i')
            ->leftJoin('i.product', 'p')
            ->where('o.user = :userId')
            ->andWhere('o.createdAt >= :since')
            ->setParameter('userId', $userId)
            ->setParameter('since', new \DateTime(\-{$days} days"))
            ->orderBy('o.createdAt'
column12: $orders = $em->getRepository(Order::class)->findAll();\n// Loads ALL orders with no filtering\n// Then filters in PHP: collection operations on 100K records

Description: Write reversible migrations with proper up and down methods
Do: Always define rollback. Add indexes in migrations. Use schema builder methods
Dont: Edit existing migrations after deployment or create irreversible migrations

Description: When using PDO directly always use prepared statements and proper error handling
Do: Use prepared statements with named parameters. Set PDO error mode to exceptions
Dont: Concatenate user input into SQL strings

Description: Create indexes based on actual query patterns for optimal read performance
Do: Index columns in WHERE JOIN and ORDER BY. Use composite indexes for multi-column queries
Dont: Add indexes on every column or ignore indexing entirely

Description: Use seeders for development data and factories for test data with proper separation
Do: Create realistic seed data. Separate seeders by entity. Use factories for randomized test data
Dont: Use seeders in production or create tightly coupled test data

Description: Configure database connections properly for different environments and workloads
Do: Use read/write splitting. Configure connection pooling. Set appropriate timeouts
Dont: Use default connection settings for production with high traffic
Code Good:
// config/database.php
'mysql' => [
    'read' => [
        'host' => [env('DB_READ_HOST_1'), env('DB_READ_HOST_2')],
    ],
    'write' => [
        'host' => [env('DB_WRITE_HOST')],
    ],
    'sticky' => true, // Read from write after writing
    'driver' => 'mysql',
    'database' => env('DB_DATABASE'),
    'charset' => 'utf8mb4',
    'collation' => 'utf8mb4_unicode_ci',
    'prefix' => '',
    'options' => [
        PDO::ATTR_PERSISTENT => true, // Connection pooling
    ],
],

Description: Monitor and optimize database queries in development and production
Do: Use Debugbar/Telescope in dev. Log slow queries. Use EXPLAIN for optimization
Dont: Deploy without monitoring queries discovering issues from user complaints
